subject: Re: Reconstruct sub-volumes/export subtomograms crash
body:
Hi Lorenzo,

Thanks for your reply. The issue was related to the format of my star file, 
or rather missing information I need to add manually. I used the PEET 
toRelionCoords command to convert particle coordinates from IMOD/PEET to 
Relion star format. Most of the key information was converted, but I needed 
to add paths to the files for generating particle stacks.

Thanks,
Josh

On Monday, 31 July 2023 at 12:47:36 UTC+1 lorenzo...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Josh,
>
> I'm not familiar with that error. This happens once you launch the 
> reconstruction? Can you share your star file to check if I see something 
> odd?
>
> Cheers,
> Lorenzo
>
> On Wed, Jul 26, 2023 at 4:11 PM Josh Jenkins <joshuaj...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi, 
>>
>> I'm following the workflow for subtomogram reconstruction in Warp (tried 
>> 1.0.9 and 1.1.0 beta). (
>> https://teamtomo.org/walkthroughs/EMPIAR-10164/relion.html). Warp 
>> crashes and produces the output below, indicating the input string is not 
>> in the correct format. I've checked the .star file and everything looks 
>> correct. Any ideas where I'm going wrong?
>>
>> Thanks,
>> Josh
>>
>> System.FormatException: Input string was not in a correct format.
